source_pr.title: executor: fix flaky test TestIndexJoin31494
source_pr.body:

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #63501 

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: When a join worker panicked, the code could cancel the context before recording the panic error, so callers might observe `context canceled` instead of the real error intermittently.
failure_signature: Index lookup hash join: panic handling can surface `context canceled` instead of the real error due to cancellation order.
root_cause_explanation: The join worker panic path cancelled other workers before recording the recovered panic error. Under concurrency, this can cause downstream code to return `context canceled` instead of the real panic error, making the test flaky. This PR records the panic error first and then cancels workers, ensuring stable error reporting.
fix_pattern: Record the recovered panic error before cancelling workers/context to avoid returning `context canceled` instead of the real error.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix flaky test TestIndexJoin31494
